# Without --use-datastar, elements/signal/script are NOT reserved: they are
# ordinary arguments. Here they are path values bound as plain strings.
muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /a/{elements}/{signal}/{script} Demo(ctx, elements, signal, script)" -}}{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.24
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// Demo takes elements, signal, and script as ordinary string path values
// because --use-datastar is not set, so the names are not reserved callbacks.
func (Server) Demo(ctx context.Context, elements, signal, script string) string {
	return elements + "-" + signal + "-" + script
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func TestDemo(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/a/e/s/c", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d", rec.Code)
	}
	if got := rec.Body.String(); got != "e-s-c" {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want %q", got, "e-s-c")
	}
}
